findings.evidence: Isotopic namespace defines of_isotopy/refl/symm/isEmbedding_left/right/homotopic/homotopicWith, no trans; roadmap README:489 `D ≈ D' ↔ Isotopic …`, :443-445 equivalence IS Isotopic, :94 define-once intent.
findings.fix: Add Isotopic.trans (and ideally an Equivalence/Setoid bundling) via Homotopy.trans for the homotopy plus a glued-total-map-embedding lemma; do not weaken the total-map-embedding definition.
findings.issue: Isotopic exposes refl and symm but omits trans, so the relation the roadmap specialises to knot equivalence (`D ≈ D' ↔ Isotopic …`, README:489; 'define once, none should re-define it', README:94) is not usable as an equivalence relation. The author's claim that trans requires weakening hypotheses (closed embeddings/Hausdorff/compactness) is incorrect: the concatenated total map preserves the time coordinate, so injectivity reduces to per-slice injectivity and both the map and its inverse are continuous by the closed pasting lemma on the two time-halves — trans holds at full generality.

summary: The homeomorph gap is fixed (isHomeomorph_final plus bundled homeomorph/finalHomeomorph). But Isotopic is advertised in-file and in the roadmap as the reusable equivalence relation underlying knot equivalence (README:489, D ≈ D' ↔ Isotopic …), and it exposes refl and @[symm] symm but no trans, so the characteristic equivalence-relation API is incomplete for its named target.
